differentials.name: Posterior deep tibiotalar ligament (PDTL) Deltoid ligament rupture is rare without additional injuries to the ankle ← uncommon occurrence of eversion ankle sprains and due to intrinsic thickness of the ligament.
differentials.raw: Posterior deep tibiotalar ligament (PDTL) Deltoid ligament rupture is rare without additional injuries to the ankle ← uncommon occurrence of eversion ankle sprains and due to intrinsic thickness of the ligament.

context: Ligamentous Stabilizers of Ankle Deltoid Ligament =  MEDIAL COLLATERAL LIGAMENT OF ANKLE Function:   main stabilizer against valgus force / pronation force / rotational force on talus Components: (a)  superficial layer: Origin:   anterior colliculus of medial malleolus Distal attachment:   calcaneus Distal attachment:   os naviculare Distal attachment:   talus Distal attachment:   joins superomedial oblique band of spring lig. proper (= calcaneonavicular lig.) Function:   stabilizer for talocalcaneonavicular joint + medial plantar arch (b)  deep layer = intraarticular, covered by synovium Orig
